phase_statuses.title: Define workbook contract and validation model
phase_statuses.objective: Replace the current implicit demo data shape with an explicit workbook-backed configuration contract for company home, showrooms, and products.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: Parser tests and reviewed workbook schema documentation recorded in `execution-log.md`.

acceptance_criteria.text: The codebase has a single documented configuration contract that covers company home, showrooms, and products, including Chinese and English media/text fields.
Workbook import validation stops immediately with explicit errors when required fields, relations, or media references are missing.
Workbook export writes the same agreed contract so an exported file can be re-imported without schema loss.

phase_statuses.notes: - Workbook schema, runtime validation, and round-trip import/export contract implemented in src/config-schema.js, src/config-workbook.js, and src/demoData.js.

phase_statuses.title: Build admin console import/export flow
phase_statuses.objective: Add an admin UI surface that can import a workbook into runtime state and export the current state back to the agreed workbook shape.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: Focused unit tests plus browser evidence of import/export actions recorded in `execution-log.md` and `test-report.md`.

acceptance_criteria.text: The frontend exposes an admin console page with one-click import and one-click export actions.
Importing a valid workbook updates runtime configuration for the current session without manual code edits.
Import failures surface explicit validation errors and do not partially apply invalid data.

phase_statuses.notes: - Admin console import/export flow implemented in src/app.js and src/admin-console.js with explicit error handling and file download support.

phase_statuses.title: Generate homepage, showroom, and product views from config
phase_statuses.objective: Render the visitor-facing frontend from imported configuration instead of hardcoded content.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: Rendering tests and Playwright screenshots or traces recorded in `execution-log.md` and `test-report.md`.

acceptance_criteria.text: The company homepage image and bilingual media/text fields render from imported configuration.
Showroom names, showroom images, product lists, and product media/text fields render from imported configuration.
The visitor-facing UI no longer requires hardcoded showroom/product content to display the configured experience.

phase_statuses.notes: - Homepage, showroom view, and product detail rendering now come from imported config in src/app.js and src/demoData.js.

phase_statuses.title: Verify with strict TDD evidence and real-browser coverage
phase_statuses.objective: Add regression coverage and browser proof for the workbook-driven experience.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: Verification commands and browser evidence recorded in `execution-log.md` and `test-report.md`.

acceptance_criteria.text: Strict TDD evidence exists in `execution-log.md` with at least one RED and matching GREEN entry for the new config-driven behavior.
`npm test -- --run`, `npm run build`, and `npx playwright test` pass for the delivered scope.
The final test report includes independent browser verification evidence for the admin flow and the generated visitor-facing pages.

phase_statuses.notes: - Vitest, Vite build, Playwright browser coverage, and frontend feature evidence all passed for the workbook-driven flow.

goal: Scheduled admin console page with workbook import/export and config-driven company home, showroom, and product rendering.
